socscThis finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-09-19 18:38:47 UTC.12/07/2018
This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor) >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Cornell University Office of the Judicial Administrator records
Cornell University. Office of the Judicial Administrator
34-10-1666
4.5 cubic feet. 4.5 cubic feet.
1871-2014. Includes minutes of Judiciary Board meetings; correspondence; newspaper clippings; material concerning student protest, 1970; records of the Student Conduct Committee, 1931-1968; Student Code, 1871-1959; and other records of the Men's Judiciary Board, the Undergraduate Judiciary Board, and the Freshman Judiciary Board at Cornell University. Collection material in English 2073989
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
Evan Earle, Janette Park
Date completed:
March 2018
EAD encoding:
RMC Staff, March 2018
Date modified:
Fredrika Loew, March 2018
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Includes minutes of Judiciary Board meetings; correspondence; newspaper clippings; material concerning student protest, 1970; records of the Student Conduct Committee, 1931-1968; Student Code, 1871-1959; and other records of the Men's Judiciary Board, the Undergraduate Judiciary Board, and the Freshman Judiciary Board at Cornell University.
Cite As:
Cornell University Office of the Judicial Administrator Records, #34-10-1666. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Access Restrictions:
Access restricted to permission of office of origin with the exception of Box 5 (annual reports).
Access Restrictions:
Access to individual case files restricted to permission of student or 80 years after the creation of the record.
College Students -- Conduct of life. College discipline. Cornell University -- : Students.
